perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that discussions and related information shared between the Parties will not be used as a basis to place AST or its members on notice (e.g., for purposes of willfulness, inducement or otherwise) of the patents and patent applications which are offered for sale.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that discussions and related information shared between the Parties will not be used as a basis to place AST on notice of the patents and patent applications which are offered for sale.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "or its members" in Section 6, the agreement now lacks clarity on whether the information shared can be used to put AST's members on notice, creating an in-text contradiction. The original clause intended to protect both AST and its members from being put on notice, but the omission suggests that while AST is protected, its members might not be. This omission introduces uncertainty and potential legal challenges regarding the scope of protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree that discussions and related information shared between the Parties will not be used as a basis to place AST on notice of the patents and patent applications which are offered for sale.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'discussions and related information shared between the Parties will not be used as a basis to place AST or its members on notice'. The changed text removes 'or its members', stating only 'The Parties agree that discussions and related information shared between the Parties will not be used as a basis to place AST on notice'. The contradicted text is the same as the original text. Since the changed text now only protects AST, it creates an in-text contradiction because, by omission, it implies the members are not protected, while the original text and contradicted text state that the members are protected.

perturbation.original_text: Whenever the permission or consent of either Party is required or permitted under this Agreement, such consent will not unreasonably be withheld, delayed or made subject to any condition not specifically provided for in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Whenever the permission or consent of either Party is required or permitted under this Agreement, such consent will not unreasonably be withheld or delayed.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "or made subject to any condition not specifically provided for in this Agreement" in Section 13, the agreement now implies that consent can be unreasonably conditioned, creating an in-text contradiction. The purpose of the original clause was to ensure the parties acted reasonably and did not impose conditions that were not part of the NDA, but the omission suggests that unreasonable conditions might now be permissible. This impacts the overall intent of the agreement, allowing conditions beyond those explicitly agreed upon and weakening mutual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Whenever the permission or consent of either Party is required or permitted under this Agreement, such consent will not unreasonably be withheld or delayed; but this Agreement, as amended by such deletion, will continue in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the phrase 'or made subject to any condition not specifically provided for in this Agreement.' This omission directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement, which aimed to ensure parties acted reasonably and didn't impose conditions beyond those explicitly agreed upon. By removing this limitation, the agreement now implies that consent can be unreasonably conditioned, thus creating a contradiction with the contradicted text which assumes such unreasonable conditions are impermissible under the agreement. The omission weakens mutual obligations and impacts the overall intent, causing a clear contradiction.
